stories.1: I’m a 911 operator, and I just received a call from a number that hasn't existed since 1994.
stories.2: My daughter has an imaginary friend, but today she drew a portrait of him and it looks exactly like my missing brother.
stories.3: I work the night shift at a morgue, and one of the John Does just asked for a glass of water.
stories.4: The GPS on my phone keeps trying to route me to a cemetery, even when I turn the phone off.
stories.5: I found a hidden room in my new apartment, and it's filled with photos of me sleeping.
stories.6: My wife has been dead for five years, but her Fitbit just logged 10,000 steps today.
stories.7: I accepted a job with a simple rule: 'Do not look at the ceiling', but I just heard a drip.
stories.8: Every night at 3:00 AM, my Alexa starts laughing in my deceased mother's voice.
stories.9: I’m a deep-sea welder, and I just saw something knock on the metal from the inside of the pipeline.
stories.10: I bought a used car, and the radio only plays news broadcasts from tomorrow.
stories.11: My dog barks at the closet every night, but tonight something barked back.
stories.12: I found a journal in the walls of my house, and the last entry is dated today: 'He found the journal.'
stories.13: I’m a teacher, and all my students drew the exact same black sun in art class today.
stories.14: My reflection just blinked three seconds after I did.
stories.15: I live in a gated community, but the guards say no one has entered or left in three days.
stories.16: I woke up with a surgical scar on my stomach that wasn't there when I went to sleep.
stories.17: The man standing under the streetlamp has moved one step closer every hour for the last three nights.
stories.18: I received a letter from myself, written in my handwriting, warning me not to open the basement door.
stories.19: My twin brother died in the womb, but sometimes I feel two heartbeats in my chest.
stories.20: I’m an air traffic controller, and a plane just requested to land that went missing 20 years ago.
stories.21: The scarecrow in the field across the road changes its pose every time lightning flashes.
stories.22: I found a USB drive sewn into the lining of my favorite coat, and it contains videos of my childhood I've never seen.
stories.23: My smart home locked me inside, and the thermostat is slowly rising.
stories.24: I work at a suicide hotline, and I just got a call from my own cell phone number.
stories.25: The baby monitor is picking up a lullaby, but my wife and I are both in the living room.
stories.26: I’m a park ranger, and we have a rule: If you see the stairs in the woods, do not climb them.
stories.27: My cat has been staring at the empty corner for hours, and now he's hissing.
stories.28: I woke up in a coffin, but my phone still has service.
stories.29: The painting I bought at the flea market changes every time I leave the room.
stories.30: I’m a pilot, and there is a passenger in seat 14C that no one else can see.
stories.31: My son told me the 'bad man' lives in the mirror, and today I saw a hand press against the glass from the other side.
stories.32: I received a text message from my dad, but we buried him with his phone yesterday.
stories.33: The elevator in my building goes to a 13th floor that isn't on the button panel.
stories.34: I’m a janitor at a high school, and the lockers are banging shut one by one down the hallway.
stories.35: I found a set of tapes in my attic labeled with my family's names, but one tape is labeled 'The Replacement'.
stories.36: My wife is sleepwalking again, but this time she's holding a knife and speaking Latin.
stories.37: I live alone, but I just found a warm cup of coffee on my kitchen counter.
stories.38: The fog outside hasn't lifted in four days, and I hear screaming in the distance.
stories.39: I’m a truck driver, and I've been driving on this straight road for six hours without seeing a single turn.
stories.40: My new house has a door that can only be opened from the outside.
stories.41: I found a list of rules for surviving my night shift, and rule #1 is 'Don't trust the manager'.
stories.42: The mannequins in the department store are facing a different direction than they were five minutes ago.
stories.43: I’m a diver, and I found a submerged house with the lights still on.
stories.44: My reflection is crying, but I'm not.
stories.45: I received an amber alert for my own child, who is sitting right next to me.
stories.46: The trees in the forest behind my house are moving closer to the windows.
stories.47: I work in a secure facility, and something just breached the containment unit.
stories.48: My neighbor has been digging a hole in his backyard every night for a week, and it's getting big enough for a body.
stories.49: I found a camera hidden in the smoke detector of my Airbnb.
stories.50: The stranger on the subway knows my name, my address, and my biggest fear.
stories.51: I’m a locksmith, and I was called to open a door that was locked from the inside, but the room was empty.
stories.52: My phone's facial recognition unlocked for the empty space behind me.
stories.53: I found a diary that narrates my life in real-time.
stories.54: The shadows in my room are getting longer, even though the light source hasn't moved.
stories.55: I’m a nurse, and a patient who has been in a coma for ten years just grabbed my wrist.
stories.56: The ice cream truck plays a distorted version of 'Pop Goes the Weasel' at 2:00 AM.
stories.57: I found a tooth in my food, but it's a human molar.
stories.58: My reflection didn't mimic my movement when I raised my hand.
stories.59: I’m a security guard, and the cameras show someone walking through the halls, but the sensors aren't tripping.
stories.60: The doll I threw away in the dumpster is back on my daughter's bed.
stories.61: I received a package with no return address containing a lock of my own hair.
stories.62: My roommate has been acting strange, and today I saw him unhinge his jaw.
stories.63: I work at a lighthouse, and something massive just swam past the light.
stories.64: The whispering in the walls stops when I press my ear against them.
stories.65: I found a secret tunnel in the basement of the library.
stories.66: My son's imaginary friend is telling him to burn the house down.
stories.67: I’m a graveyard shift worker, and the graves are starting to open.
stories.68: The GPS led me to a ghost town that isn't on any map.
stories.69: I found a phone in the woods, and it's full of pictures of me.
stories.70: My wife's eyes turned black for a second when she was angry.
stories.71: I’m a pizza delivery guy, and I just delivered to a house that burned down ten years ago.
stories.72: The clown at the birthday party isn't blinking, and he hasn't moved in an hour.
stories.73: I found a tape recorder under my bed, and it has hours of me talking in my sleep to someone.
stories.74: My dog is growling at something floating above the ceiling fan.
stories.75: I’m a zookeeper, and the animals are all staring at one specific spot in the enclosure.
stories.76: The old radio in the attic turned on by itself and started broadcasting numbers.
stories.77: I found a photo of myself in a history book from 1920.
stories.78: My reflection is holding a sign that says 'Help Me'.
stories.79: I’m a cleaner, and I found a bloody handprint on the ceiling.
stories.80: The wind is howling my name.
stories.81: I found a box of teeth in the crawlspace.
stories.82: My new neighbor never leaves his house, but I see silhouettes dancing in the windows.
stories.83: I’m a fisherman, and I pulled up a net full of human bones.
stories.84: The scarecrow is closer than it was yesterday.
stories.85: I found a message written in steam on the bathroom mirror: 'Run'.
stories.86: My car broke down in the middle of nowhere, and I hear footsteps approaching.
stories.87: I’m a bartender, and a customer just ordered a drink that hasn't been made in a century.
stories.88: The lights in the hallway are flickering in Morse code.
stories.89: I found a voodoo doll that looks exactly like me, and it has a pin in the heart.
stories.90: My reflection grinned when I was crying.
stories.91: I’m a taxi driver, and my passenger just vanished from the back seat.
stories.92: The birds have stopped singing, and the silence is deafening.
stories.93: I found a key inside a loaf of bread.
stories.94: My phone is typing a message on its own.
stories.95: I’m a hotel receptionist, and a guest just checked in who looks exactly like the person on the missing poster.
stories.96: The water in the bathtub turned to blood.
stories.97: I found a trapdoor under the rug in the living room.
stories.98: My shadow is moving independently of me.
stories.99: I’m a forest ranger, and I found a staircase in the middle of the woods.
stories.100: The painting of the old lady is watching me.
stories.101: I signed up for a sleep study, but I've been awake for 72 hours and the doctors won't let me leave.
stories.102: My robotic vacuum keeps trying to enter the locked basement.
stories.103: I’m an Uber driver, and my passenger just told me he’s going to 'the end of the line', but we’re on a dead-end street.
stories.104: The new update on my phone installed an app I can't delete, and it's counting down.
stories.105: I found a diary in a thrift store, and the handwriting changes from frantic scribbles to perfect calligraphy halfway through.
stories.106: My grandmother with dementia just spoke to me in perfect French, a language she never learned.
stories.107: I’m a librarian, and the books in the occult section are rearranging themselves.
stories.108: The strange noise in the attic sounds like someone dragging a heavy chain.
stories.109: I found a roll of film in the woods, and the developed pictures show me sleeping in my tent.
stories.110: My twin sister disappeared ten years ago, but I just saw her on the news in the background of a live report.
stories.111: I’m a construction worker, and we just hit a metal wall buried 50 feet underground.
stories.112: The humming noise in my apartment is getting louder, and my nose starts bleeding when it peaks.
stories.113: I found a strange creature washed up on the beach, and it has human eyes.
stories.114: My smart fridge ordered 50 pounds of raw meat without my permission.
stories.115: I’m a train conductor, and we just passed a station that doesn't exist.
stories.116: The scarecrow is now standing on my porch.
stories.117: I found a wedding ring inside a fish I caught, and it has my wife's initials.
stories.119: I’m a night watchman, and the mannequins are definitely moving.
stories.120: The voice on the other end of the phone sounds exactly like me.
stories.121: I found a hidden camera in the eye of my daughter's doll.
stories.122: My dog is barking at the wall, and now the wall is bleeding.
stories.123: I’m a lifeguard, and I just saw a hand reach up from the deep end.
stories.124: The text message says 'Look behind you', but I'm alone in a glass elevator.
stories.125: I found a map of my house in a stranger's car.
stories.127: I’m a flight attendant, and the pilot just announced we're going to a place 'where we won't be found'.
stories.128: The music box starts playing every time I talk about the accident.
stories.129: I found a jar of fingernails in the crawlspace.
stories.130: My computer webcam light turned on, and a chat window opened saying 'I see you'.
stories.131: I’m a cave explorer, and my rope just got cut from above.
stories.132: The stranger in the park is wearing the same clothes as me.
stories.133: I found a diary that predicts the future, and the next entry is about my death.
stories.134: My reflection is mimicking my movements, but with a slight delay.
stories.135: I’m a chef, and the meat supplier sent something that definitely isn't beef.
stories.136: The lights in the house are pulsing to the beat of a heart.
stories.137: I found a secret room behind the bookcase.
stories.138: My phone is recording audio, but I didn't press record.
stories.139: I’m a mail carrier, and I keep delivering letters to a house that burned down years ago.
stories.140: The doll's head is turned 180 degrees.
stories.141: I found a bloody knife in my dishwasher, and I live alone.
stories.142: My reflection is staring at me with hatred.
stories.143: I’m a bus driver, and the last passenger won't get off.
stories.144: The whispering in the wind is getting louder.
stories.145: I found a human tooth in my apple.
stories.146: My shadow is trying to strangle me.
stories.147: I’m a firefighter, and the fire seems to be avoiding one specific room.
stories.148: The painting's eyes are following me.
stories.149: I found a key that unlocks nothing in my house.
stories.151: I used a sleep tracking app, and it recorded a voice whispering 'He's finally asleep' at 3 AM.
stories.152: My commute is usually 20 minutes, but today it took 4 hours and I don't remember any of it.
stories.153: I work at a daycare, and one of the kids drew a picture of me dead.
stories.154: The new house came with a set of rules, and rule #4 is 'Ignore the crying from the basement'.
stories.155: I found a locked smartphone on the subway; I unlocked it with '1234' and it opened to my photo gallery.
stories.156: My wife has been replacing our family photos with pictures of strangers, and she hasn't noticed.
stories.157: I’m a night shift security guard, and the motion sensors are tracking something invisible moving towards me.
stories.158: I bought a vintage mirror, and sometimes I see people standing behind me in it.
stories.159: My dog is afraid of me since I came back from my hiking trip.
stories.160: I found a trapdoor in my closet that leads to a duplicate of my bedroom, but everything is rotten.
stories.161: I’m a 911 dispatcher, and I just got a call from a woman claiming to be trapped in my own basement.
stories.162: The text messages from my stalker are coming from inside the house.
stories.163: I woke up and my bedroom door was bricked over.
stories.164: I work in a mannequin factory, and we’re missing five bodies today.
stories.165: My son talks to the 'angels' in his room, but they tell him to hurt people.
stories.166: I found a VHS tape labeled 'My Birthday', but the footage is of me sleeping last night.
stories.167: The elevator stopped between floors, and something is prying the doors open from the outside.
stories.168: I’m a cleaner for crime scenes, and I just arrived at a house where the crime hasn't happened yet.
stories.169: My reflection is mouthing words I can't hear.
stories.170: I found a notebook in the woods filled with drawings of my eyes.
stories.171: The scratching in the walls is spelling out my name.
stories.172: I’m a deep sea fisherman, and we pulled up something that screamed.
stories.173: My smart speaker just announced 'Intruder alert' but the alarm didn't go off.
stories.174: I found a human bone in my backyard while gardening.
stories.175: The stranger at the door says he's my future son.
stories.176: My reflection is bleeding from the eyes.
stories.177: I’m a nurse, and the newborn babies are all silent tonight.
stories.178: The painting in the hallway is actually a window.
stories.179: I found a set of keys in my pocket that don't belong to me.
stories.181: I’m a truck driver, and the hitchhiker I picked up just vanished.
stories.182: The water in the shower smells like sulfur.
stories.183: I found a doll that looks like my dead sister.
stories.184: My reflection is laughing at me.
stories.185: I’m a security guard, and the animatronics are moving on their own.
stories.186: The whispering is coming from under the bed.
stories.187: I found a message in a bottle, and it's a plea for help from me.
stories.188: My phone is hot to the touch, and it's making strange noises.
stories.189: I’m a pilot, and the clouds are forming shapes of faces.
stories.190: The stranger in the mirror is not me.
stories.191: I found a bloody footprint in the kitchen.
stories.192: My reflection is holding a knife.
stories.193: I’m a doctor, and the patient's heart is on the wrong side.
stories.194: The lights are flickering in rhythm with my heartbeat.
stories.195: I found a secret passage in the library.
stories.196: My shadow is trying to escape.
stories.197: I’m a police officer, and the suspect disappeared from the interrogation room.
stories.198: The doll is sitting in a different chair.
stories.199: I found a tape recorder with my voice on it.
stories.201: I live in a lighthouse, and the fog just turned a violent shade of purple.
stories.202: My GPS says 'You have arrived', but I'm in the middle of a dense forest.
stories.203: I checked into a hotel room, and the previous guest is still under the bed.
stories.204: The scars on my body are forming a map.
stories.205: I’m a forensic analyst, and the fingerprints on the weapon belong to a man who died in 1890.
stories.206: My neighbor is obsessively building a fallout shelter, and he just asked if I want a spot.
stories.207: I found a polaroid of me sleeping, tucked into my windshield wiper.
stories.208: The emergency broadcast system is playing a recording of my family screaming.
stories.209: I’m a curator at a museum, and the mummy has changed positions.
stories.210: My reflection just winked at me.
stories.211: I found a hidden microphone in my bedside lamp.
stories.212: The crows are gathering on my roof, and they are all staring at me.
stories.213: I’m a subway driver, and there's a man on the tracks who won't move.
stories.214: My smart watch says I've been asleep for 3 years.
stories.215: I found a diary in the attic that details my murder.
stories.216: The stranger on the bus is sketching me.
stories.217: I’m a clown, and the kids are terrified of something behind me.
stories.218: My reflection is holding a sign that says 'Don't turn around'.
stories.219: I found a bloody shirt in my laundry basket.
stories.220: The wind chime is ringing, but there is no wind.
stories.221: I’m a gardener, and the roses are bleeding.
stories.222: My phone just sent a text to everyone in my contacts: 'Help me'.
stories.223: I found a human finger in my soup.
stories.224: The stranger in the elevator is humming my favorite song.
stories.225: I’m a librarian, and a book just fell off the shelf open to a page about demons.
stories.227: I found a key to a safety deposit box I didn't know I had.
stories.228: The lights in the tunnel are going out one by one.
stories.229: I’m a taxi driver, and the destination is a graveyard.
stories.230: My shadow is longer than it should be.
stories.231: I found a message carved into the tree: 'I'm watching you'.
stories.232: The stranger in the park is holding a picture of me.
stories.233: I’m a baker, and the dough is rising too fast.
stories.234: My reflection is missing an eye.
stories.235: I found a gun in my mailbox.
stories.236: The whispering is coming from the closet.
stories.237: I’m a teacher, and the chalkboard is writing by itself.
stories.238: My phone is displaying a countdown.
stories.239: I found a dead bird on my pillow.
stories.240: The stranger is wearing a mask made of human skin.
stories.241: I’m a lifeguard, and the pool is filled with blood.
stories.243: I found a note in my pocket: 'Don't go home'.
stories.244: The stranger is whistling a tune only I know.
stories.245: I’m a doctor, and the X-ray shows something living inside the patient.
stories.246: My shadow is reaching for me.
stories.247: I found a bloody handprint on the window.
stories.248: The stranger is standing in my backyard.
stories.249: I’m a pilot, and the sky is cracking.
stories.251: I bought a used RV, and the GPS history is just a list of unsolved murder locations.
stories.252: I’m a park ranger, and I just found a staircase in the woods that goes down, not up.
stories.253: My hearing aid is picking up a frequency that sounds like people screaming.
stories.254: I found a hidden folder on my work computer labeled 'Employee Termination Protocols: Lethal'.
stories.255: The mirror in the hotel bathroom has a two-way glass, and I see a red light behind it.
stories.256: I’m a commercial diver, and I just found a sunken ship that went missing yesterday... in 1902.
stories.257: My dog dug up a time capsule in the yard, but it contains items from next year.
stories.258: I received a letter from the government saying I was declared dead three days ago.
stories.259: I’m a night janitor at a wax museum, and the figures change expressions when I turn my back.
stories.260: My smart glasses are highlighting people in red with the tag 'Hostile'.
stories.261: I found a secret room in the subway tunnels.
stories.262: The stranger in the coffee shop has no reflection.
stories.263: I’m a butcher, and the meat is twitching.
stories.264: My reflection is begging for mercy.
stories.265: I found a tape of my parents discussing which child to keep.
stories.266: The stranger is holding a knife behind his back.
stories.267: I’m a florist, and the flowers are wilting as I touch them.
stories.268: My phone is making static noises.
stories.269: I found a human eye in my drink.
stories.270: The stranger is tapping on the window.
stories.271: I’m a mechanic, and the car has teeth in the engine.
stories.272: My reflection is covered in bugs.
stories.273: I found a note sewed into my skin.
stories.274: The stranger is singing a lullaby.
stories.275: I’m a plumber, and the pipes are screaming.
stories.277: I found a jar of hearts in the basement.
stories.278: The stranger is pointing at me.
stories.279: I’m a scientist, and the experiment has escaped.
stories.281: I found a key in my stomach.
stories.283: I’m a astronaut, and the earth is gone.
stories.284: My phone is showing a video of me dying.
stories.285: I found a bloody message on the ceiling.
stories.286: The stranger is wearing my clothes.
stories.287: I’m a librarian, and the books are bleeding.
stories.289: I found a severed head in the fridge.
stories.290: The stranger is standing at the foot of my bed.
stories.291: I’m a zookeeper, and the lions are afraid of me.
stories.292: My shadow is eating the light.
stories.293: I found a diary written in blood.
stories.294: The stranger is whispering in my ear.
stories.295: I’m a doctor, and the patient is missing his brain.
stories.297: I found a bomb in my car.
stories.298: The stranger is holding my hand.
stories.299: I’m a writer, and the story is coming true.
stories.300: The user reading this is the next victim.
